numerical_facts.context: Blue Bell ice cream has temporarily shut down one of its manufacturing plants over the discovery of listeria contamination in a serving of ice cream originating from that plant.
That includes 3-ounce servings of Blue Bell ice cream from this plant that went to institutions in containers marked with the letters O, P, Q, R, S or T behind the coding date.
The warning by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention does not affect other Blue Bell ice cream, including other 3-ounce servings, not made at the plant.
It is the third time Blue Bell has taken action in light of a listeria outbreak at a Kansas hospital that served the company's ice cream.

In a separate outbreak in Texas, the CDC did find that listeria samples taken from patients who came down with listeriosis between 2010 and 2014 in a hospital that served 3-ounce Blue Bell cups matched the listeria in recovered samples.
It later added 3-ounce cup servings to the recall.
Five people were infected and three died in the past year in Kansas from listeria that might be linked to Blue Bell Creameries products, according to the CDC.
Five people were infected and three died in the past year in Kansas from listeria that might be linked to Blue Bell Creameries products, according to the CDC.
All five of them were hospitalized at the same hospital before developing listeriosis, the CDC said.
At least four of them had consumed milkshakes made with Blue Bell ice cream before developing the infection.
In a statement on its website, Blue Bell said "this recall in no way includes Blue Bell ice cream half gallons, pints, quarts, 3 gallons or other 3 oz. cups."
In a statement on its website, Blue Bell said "this recall in no way includes Blue Bell ice cream half gallons, pints, quarts, 3 gallons or other 3 oz. cups."
This has been the first product recall in the 108-year history of Blue Bell Creameries, the company said.
This has been the first product recall in the 108-year history of Blue Bell Creameries, the company said.
In the United States, an estimated 1,600 people become seriously ill each year, and approximately 16% of these illnesses result in death.

Cervical infections caused by listeriosis in pregnant women may result in stillbirth or spontaneous abortion during the second or third trimesters.

numerical_facts.context: Blue Bell ice cream has temporarily shut down one of its manufacturing plants over the discovery of listeria contamination in a serving of ice cream originating from that plant.
That includes 3-ounce servings of Blue Bell ice cream from this plant that went to institutions in containers marked with the letters O, P, Q, R, S or T behind the coding date.
The warning by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention does not affect other Blue Bell ice cream, including other 3-ounce servings, not made at the plant.
It is the third time Blue Bell has taken action in light of a listeria outbreak at a Kansas hospital that served the company's ice cream.

In a separate outbreak in Texas, the CDC did find that listeria samples taken from patients who came down with listeriosis between 2010 and 2014 in a hospital that served 3-ounce Blue Bell cups matched the listeria in recovered samples.
It later added 3-ounce cup servings to the recall.
Five people were infected and three died in the past year in Kansas from listeria that might be linked to Blue Bell Creameries products, according to the CDC.
Five people were infected and three died in the past year in Kansas from listeria that might be linked to Blue Bell Creameries products, according to the CDC.
All five of them were hospitalized at the same hospital before developing listeriosis, the CDC said.
At least four of them had consumed milkshakes made with Blue Bell ice cream before developing the infection.
In a statement on its website, Blue Bell said "this recall in no way includes Blue Bell ice cream half gallons, pints, quarts, 3 gallons or other 3 oz. cups."
In a statement on its website, Blue Bell said "this recall in no way includes Blue Bell ice cream half gallons, pints, quarts, 3 gallons or other 3 oz. cups."
This has been the first product recall in the 108-year history of Blue Bell Creameries, the company said.
This has been the first product recall in the 108-year history of Blue Bell Creameries, the company said.
In the United States, an estimated 1,600 people become seriously ill each year, and approximately 16% of these illnesses result in death.
In the United States, an estimated 1,600 people become seriously ill each year, and approximately 16% of these illnesses result in death.
Cervical infections caused by listeriosis in pregnant women may result in stillbirth or spontaneous abortion during the second or third trimesters.
Cervical infections caused by listeriosis in pregnant women may result in stillbirth or spontaneous abortion during the second or third trimesters.

temporal_facts.context: Public health officials warned consumers Friday not to eat any Blue Bell-branded products made at the company's Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, plant.
The cup contaminated with the bacteria was produced at the Broken Arrow plant in April 2014, Blue Bell said.

In a separate outbreak in Texas, the CDC did find that listeria samples taken from patients who came down with listeriosis between 2010 and 2014 in a hospital that served 3-ounce Blue Bell cups matched the listeria in recovered samples.

In a separate outbreak in Texas, the CDC did find that listeria samples taken from patients who came down with listeriosis between 2010 and 2014 in a hospital that served 3-ounce Blue Bell cups matched the listeria in recovered samples.
In early March, in light of the Kansas listeria outbreak, Blue Bell recalled a group of products made at a plant in Texas.
Five people were infected and three died in the past year in Kansas from listeria that might be linked to Blue Bell Creameries products, according to the CDC.
In the United States, an estimated 1,600 people become seriously ill each year, and approximately 16% of these illnesses result in death.

numerical_facts.context: Blue Bell ice cream has temporarily shut down one of its manufacturing plants over the discovery of listeria contamination in a serving of ice cream originating from that plant.
That includes 3-ounce servings of Blue Bell ice cream from this plant that went to institutions in containers marked with the letters O, P, Q, R, S or T behind the coding date.
The warning by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention does not affect other Blue Bell ice cream, including other 3-ounce servings, not made at the plant.
It is the third time Blue Bell has taken action in light of a listeria outbreak at a Kansas hospital that served the company's ice cream.

In a separate outbreak in Texas, the CDC did find that listeria samples taken from patients who came down with listeriosis between 2010 and 2014 in a hospital that served 3-ounce Blue Bell cups matched the listeria in recovered samples.
It later added 3-ounce cup servings to the recall.
Five people were infected and three died in the past year in Kansas from listeria that might be linked to Blue Bell Creameries products, according to the CDC.
Five people were infected and three died in the past year in Kansas from listeria that might be linked to Blue Bell Creameries products, according to the CDC.
All five of them were hospitalized at the same hospital before developing listeriosis, the CDC said.
At least four of them had consumed milkshakes made with Blue Bell ice cream before developing the infection.

explicit_negations.statement: True statements are not defamatory.

explicit_negations.context: But the Rolling Stone article certainly purported to be fact, and it apparently is not exactly what the law considers "true."

explicit_negations.statement: Individual members of the fraternity will not be considered public figures.
explicit_negations.context: The individual members of the fraternity will likely be considered private individuals, and not public figures; the latter have a harder time proving defamation.

explicit_negations.statement: The government cannot sue for defamation.
explicit_negations.context: The Supreme Court has been clear on the issue of libelous statements about the government: The government cannot sue for defamation.

explicit_negations.context: There is no such cause of action in American jurisprudence.

explicit_negations.statement: A fraternity is not an individual.
explicit_negations.context: A fraternity is not an individual, but a group.

explicit_negations.statement: Groups generally cannot sue for defamation.

explicit_negations.statement: Defamatory statements about a group are not actionable by the group's individual members, for the most part.
explicit_negations.context: Defamatory statements about a group are therefore not actionable by the group's individual members, for the most part.

numerical_facts.describes: rank of the woman to serve in the body
numerical_facts.context: When I was elected to the Kentucky State Senate in 1967, I became the first woman and the first person of color to serve in the body.

numerical_facts.describes: rank of the person of color to serve in the body
numerical_facts.context: When I was elected to the Kentucky State Senate in 1967, I became the first woman and the first person of color to serve in the body.

Five decades later, I find it almost unfathomable that a politician from my own state is attempting to launch his presidential campaign on a record that includes questioning landmark voting rights and civil rights legislation.

numerical_facts.describes: existence of Department of Education in the past
numerical_facts.context: I have no doubt that under Paul's leadership, he would indeed take our country back -- in the wrong direction -- way back to a time when we were debating the Civil Rights Act -- which Paul has done since landing on the national stage; when there was no Department of Education -- a department he thinks "should be done away with;" when women didn't have choices -- choices Paul seeks to limit in Washington; when DREAMers weren't protected from deportation -- protections Paul currently opposes.

numerical_facts.describes: rank of the anniversary of Bloody Sunday
numerical_facts.context: When President Obama stood with John Lewis and other veterans of the civil rights movement in front of the Edmund Pettus Bridge last month to mark the 50th anniversary of Bloody Sunday, he inspired us all by saying: "With effort, we can roll back poverty and the roadblocks to opportunity.

numerical_facts.context: Paul tried once again from that stage in Louisville to fashion himself as the one member of his party courageous enough to try to broaden Republican appeal to constituencies they ignore year after year.

The American people deserve a leader who won't disrespect their intelligence, who won't pander to them when it's convenient, and who won't work to dismantle the progress we have made over the last five decades.

temporal_facts.context: The photographer, Daniel Ramos, had filed the civil suit against West after the hip-hop star attacked him and tried to wrestle his camera from him in July 2013 at Los Angeles International Airport.
The case had been set for trial next week, but Allred issued a statement Tuesday night saying Ramos' side had filed a dismissal "because the case was settled to the satisfaction of the parties."
"Kanye! Kanye! Talk to me, Kanye!" Ramos shouts outside a terminal at the Los Angeles airport on the night of July 19, 2013.

He was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1987, and has 30 Grammy nominations.
King, 89, has used various models of Gibson guitars over the years, and named each one of them Lucille.

numerical_facts.context: Copeland and a female companion, Katrine Saltara, were in the club for about 10 minutes before leaving and walking down the street toward Fulton Houses, where their car was parked, said a 1OAK spokesperson.

Copeland and Saltara tried to make their way back to the club to seek help from the 20 or so security personnel on hand, leaving a "bloody trail of handprints" between the site of the stabbing and the club, the spokesperson said.
A male and two females were taken to area hospitals, Ort said.
A male and two females were taken to area hospitals, Ort said.

A knife was recovered, a suspect was arrested and two individuals not involved in the dispute -- the Hawks' Pero Antic, 32, and Thabo Sefolosha, 30 -- were arrested on charges of obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, she said.

A knife was recovered, a suspect was arrested and two individuals not involved in the dispute -- the Hawks' Pero Antic, 32, and Thabo Sefolosha, 30 -- were arrested on charges of obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, she said.

Police released little information Wednesday, but local media identified the injured man as Copeland, 31, who is from Orange, New Jersey.
The pair apparently had only recently arrived in New York prior to their arrests, as both were on the court for the Hawks' 96-69 win over the Phoenix Suns in Atlanta on Tuesday night.
Antic played 12 minutes, and Sefolosha played 20.
Antic played 12 minutes, and Sefolosha played 20.
Copeland, a former Knick, was near the nightclub with Saltara when a 22-year-old Brooklyn man approached them, police told CNN affiliate WABC.
There was some sort of dispute before the suspect stabbed the 6-foot-8-inch Copeland in the abdomen, slashed Saltara and then slashed another woman, the station reported.
While WCBS reported that the second woman was 53 years old, WABC reported she was 23.
While WCBS reported that the second woman was 53 years old, WABC reported she was 23.
The Hawks are preparing for a historic playoff run after clinching the No. 1 seed in the NBA's Eastern Conference.

temporal_facts.context: Chris Copeland of the Indiana Pacers was stabbed after leaving a trendy New York nightclub early Wednesday, and two Atlanta Hawks -- who had just finished a home game hours before the incident -- were among those arrested, according to police and CNN affiliates.

Though New York Police Department Det. Kelly Ort initially told CNN the incident occurred just before 4 a.m. at 1OAK, a club in New York's Chelsea neighborhood known to draw celebrities among its clientele, the club later told CNN that the stabbing occurred in front of the Fulton Houses project down the street.
The Hawks are in New York for a Wednesday night game against the Brooklyn Nets.
Police released little information Wednesday, but local media identified the injured man as Copeland, 31, who is from Orange, New Jersey.
Copeland's Pacers are slated to play the New York Knicks on Wednesday night.
The pair apparently had only recently arrived in New York prior to their arrests, as both were on the court for the Hawks' 96-69 win over the Phoenix Suns in Atlanta on Tuesday night.
The game ended around 10 p.m.

The Hawks are in New York for a Wednesday night game against the Brooklyn Nets.
Police released little information Wednesday, but local media identified the injured man as Copeland, 31, who is from Orange, New Jersey.
Copeland's Pacers are slated to play the New York Knicks on Wednesday night.
The pair apparently had only recently arrived in New York prior to their arrests, as both were on the court for the Hawks' 96-69 win over the Phoenix Suns in Atlanta on Tuesday night.
The game ended around 10 p.m.

numerical_facts.context: Copeland and a female companion, Katrine Saltara, were in the club for about 10 minutes before leaving and walking down the street toward Fulton Houses, where their car was parked, said a 1OAK spokesperson.

Copeland and Saltara tried to make their way back to the club to seek help from the 20 or so security personnel on hand, leaving a "bloody trail of handprints" between the site of the stabbing and the club, the spokesperson said.
A male and two females were taken to area hospitals, Ort said.
A male and two females were taken to area hospitals, Ort said.

A knife was recovered, a suspect was arrested and two individuals not involved in the dispute -- the Hawks' Pero Antic, 32, and Thabo Sefolosha, 30 -- were arrested on charges of obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, she said.

A knife was recovered, a suspect was arrested and two individuals not involved in the dispute -- the Hawks' Pero Antic, 32, and Thabo Sefolosha, 30 -- were arrested on charges of obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, she said.

A knife was recovered, a suspect was arrested and two individuals not involved in the dispute -- the Hawks' Pero Antic, 32, and Thabo Sefolosha, 30 -- were arrested on charges of obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, she said.
Police released little information Wednesday, but local media identified the injured man as Copeland, 31, who is from Orange, New Jersey.
The pair apparently had only recently arrived in New York prior to their arrests, as both were on the court for the Hawks' 96-69 win over the Phoenix Suns in Atlanta on Tuesday night.
Antic played 12 minutes, and Sefolosha played 20.
Antic played 12 minutes, and Sefolosha played 20.
Copeland, a former Knick, was near the nightclub with Saltara when a 22-year-old Brooklyn man approached them, police told CNN affiliate WABC.
There was some sort of dispute before the suspect stabbed the 6-foot-8-inch Copeland in the abdomen, slashed Saltara and then slashed another woman, the station reported.
While WCBS reported that the second woman was 53 years old, WABC reported she was 23.
While WCBS reported that the second woman was 53 years old, WABC reported she was 23.
The Hawks are preparing for a historic playoff run after clinching the No. 1 seed in the NBA's Eastern Conference.

temporal_facts.context: Chris Copeland of the Indiana Pacers was stabbed after leaving a trendy New York nightclub early Wednesday, and two Atlanta Hawks -- who had just finished a home game hours before the incident -- were among those arrested, according to police and CNN affiliates.

Though New York Police Department Det. Kelly Ort initially told CNN the incident occurred just before 4 a.m. at 1OAK, a club in New York's Chelsea neighborhood known to draw celebrities among its clientele, the club later told CNN that the stabbing occurred in front of the Fulton Houses project down the street.
The Hawks are in New York for a Wednesday night game against the Brooklyn Nets.
Police released little information Wednesday, but local media identified the injured man as Copeland, 31, who is from Orange, New Jersey.
Copeland's Pacers are slated to play the New York Knicks on Wednesday night.
The pair apparently had only recently arrived in New York prior to their arrests, as both were on the court for the Hawks' 96-69 win over the Phoenix Suns in Atlanta on Tuesday night.
The game ended around 10 p.m.

numerical_facts.context: Copeland and a female companion, Katrine Saltara, were in the club for about 10 minutes before leaving and walking down the street toward Fulton Houses, where their car was parked, said a 1OAK spokesperson.

Copeland and Saltara tried to make their way back to the club to seek help from the 20 or so security personnel on hand, leaving a "bloody trail of handprints" between the site of the stabbing and the club, the spokesperson said.
A male and two females were taken to area hospitals, Ort said.
A male and two females were taken to area hospitals, Ort said.

A knife was recovered, a suspect was arrested and two individuals not involved in the dispute -- the Hawks' Pero Antic, 32, and Thabo Sefolosha, 30 -- were arrested on charges of obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, she said.

A knife was recovered, a suspect was arrested and two individuals not involved in the dispute -- the Hawks' Pero Antic, 32, and Thabo Sefolosha, 30 -- were arrested on charges of obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, she said.

A knife was recovered, a suspect was arrested and two individuals not involved in the dispute -- the Hawks' Pero Antic, 32, and Thabo Sefolosha, 30 -- were arrested on charges of obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, she said.
Police released little information Wednesday, but local media identified the injured man as Copeland, 31, who is from Orange, New Jersey.
The pair apparently had only recently arrived in New York prior to their arrests, as both were on the court for the Hawks' 96-69 win over the Phoenix Suns in Atlanta on Tuesday night.
Antic played 12 minutes, and Sefolosha played 20.
Antic played 12 minutes, and Sefolosha played 20.
Copeland, a former Knick, was near the nightclub with Saltara when a 22-year-old Brooklyn man approached them, police told CNN affiliate WABC.
There was some sort of dispute before the suspect stabbed the 6-foot-8-inch Copeland in the abdomen, slashed Saltara and then slashed another woman, the station reported.
While WCBS reported that the second woman was 53 years old, WABC reported she was 23.
While WCBS reported that the second woman was 53 years old, WABC reported she was 23.
The Hawks are preparing for a historic playoff run after clinching the No. 1 seed in the NBA's Eastern Conference.

temporal_facts.context: Chris Copeland of the Indiana Pacers was stabbed after leaving a trendy New York nightclub early Wednesday, and two Atlanta Hawks -- who had just finished a home game hours before the incident -- were among those arrested, according to police and CNN affiliates.

Though New York Police Department Det. Kelly Ort initially told CNN the incident occurred just before 4 a.m. at 1OAK, a club in New York's Chelsea neighborhood known to draw celebrities among its clientele, the club later told CNN that the stabbing occurred in front of the Fulton Houses project down the street.
The Hawks are in New York for a Wednesday night game against the Brooklyn Nets.
Police released little information Wednesday, but local media identified the injured man as Copeland, 31, who is from Orange, New Jersey.
Copeland's Pacers are slated to play the New York Knicks on Wednesday night.
The pair apparently had only recently arrived in New York prior to their arrests, as both were on the court for the Hawks' 96-69 win over the Phoenix Suns in Atlanta on Tuesday night.
The game ended around 10 p.m.

numerical_facts.context: Copeland and a female companion, Katrine Saltara, were in the club for about 10 minutes before leaving and walking down the street toward Fulton Houses, where their car was parked, said a 1OAK spokesperson.

Copeland and Saltara tried to make their way back to the club to seek help from the 20 or so security personnel on hand, leaving a "bloody trail of handprints" between the site of the stabbing and the club, the spokesperson said.
A male and two females were taken to area hospitals, Ort said.
A male and two females were taken to area hospitals, Ort said.

A knife was recovered, a suspect was arrested and two individuals not involved in the dispute -- the Hawks' Pero Antic, 32, and Thabo Sefolosha, 30 -- were arrested on charges of obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, she said.

A knife was recovered, a suspect was arrested and two individuals not involved in the dispute -- the Hawks' Pero Antic, 32, and Thabo Sefolosha, 30 -- were arrested on charges of obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, she said.

A knife was recovered, a suspect was arrested and two individuals not involved in the dispute -- the Hawks' Pero Antic, 32, and Thabo Sefolosha, 30 -- were arrested on charges of obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, she said.
Police released little information Wednesday, but local media identified the injured man as Copeland, 31, who is from Orange, New Jersey.
The pair apparently had only recently arrived in New York prior to their arrests, as both were on the court for the Hawks' 96-69 win over the Phoenix Suns in Atlanta on Tuesday night.
Antic played 12 minutes, and Sefolosha played 20.
Antic played 12 minutes, and Sefolosha played 20.
Copeland, a former Knick, was near the nightclub with Saltara when a 22-year-old Brooklyn man approached them, police told CNN affiliate WABC.
There was some sort of dispute before the suspect stabbed the 6-foot-8-inch Copeland in the abdomen, slashed Saltara and then slashed another woman, the station reported.
While WCBS reported that the second woman was 53 years old, WABC reported she was 23.
While WCBS reported that the second woman was 53 years old, WABC reported she was 23.
The Hawks are preparing for a historic playoff run after clinching the No. 1 seed in the NBA's Eastern Conference.

temporal_facts.context: Chris Copeland of the Indiana Pacers was stabbed after leaving a trendy New York nightclub early Wednesday, and two Atlanta Hawks -- who had just finished a home game hours before the incident -- were among those arrested, according to police and CNN affiliates.

Though New York Police Department Det. Kelly Ort initially told CNN the incident occurred just before 4 a.m. at 1OAK, a club in New York's Chelsea neighborhood known to draw celebrities among its clientele, the club later told CNN that the stabbing occurred in front of the Fulton Houses project down the street.
The Hawks are in New York for a Wednesday night game against the Brooklyn Nets.
Police released little information Wednesday, but local media identified the injured man as Copeland, 31, who is from Orange, New Jersey.
Copeland's Pacers are slated to play the New York Knicks on Wednesday night.
The pair apparently had only recently arrived in New York prior to their arrests, as both were on the court for the Hawks' 96-69 win over the Phoenix Suns in Atlanta on Tuesday night.
The game ended around 10 p.m.

temporal_facts.context: Tejano star Selena, who died 20 years ago, is coming back in a big way: with a hologram-like figure.
Selena: 20 years after her death.
Twenty years after she was killed by her fan club president, Selena remains incredibly popular, with her Facebook page recording 2 million likes and fans continuing to post videos and tributes.
Selena the One "will release new songs and videos, will collaborate with current hit artists, and aims to go on tour in 2018," said a statement on Selena's Facebook page.
A Michael Jackson hologram appeared at the Billboard Music Awards in 2014, and a hologram of Tupac Shakur performed at Coachella in 2012.
A Michael Jackson hologram appeared at the Billboard Music Awards in 2014, and a hologram of Tupac Shakur performed at Coachella in 2012.
"This is something that we're building for another two to three years, so when 2018 comes around they'll be like, 'Oh, OK, we get it.' "
The campaign, which hopes to raise $500,000, begins April 16.
The commemorative Fiesta de la Flor in Corpus Christi, Texas -- which celebrates her life -- is scheduled for April 17 and 18.

explicit_negations.context: It's not about replacing Selena in any shape, way or form; it's just something to help her legacy continue growing.
